name: Reorder Point (basic)
description: The inventory level at which a replenishment order should be placed: expected demand during the lead time plus safety stock.

outputs.name: Reorder point
outputs.description: Order when on-hand inventory position reaches this level.

assumptions: Demand and lead time use the same time unit.
Average daily demand is stable over the lead time.
Safety stock is supplied separately (see inventory.safety_stock.service_level).
limitations: Deterministic form; when demand or lead time vary, size SS with the service-level method.
Not a min/max system; the max level is computed separately.

examples.note: Definitive Guide example: 10 units/day over a 4-day lead time, no safety stock.

citation.quote: the sales during the lead time is 10 units per day times 4 days or 40 units ... the reorder point is 40 units (ROP = 40).

examples.note: Garden-centre example: reorder level = average demand during lead time + safety stock.

citation.quote: Reorder level = Average demand during lead time + safety stock = 10 x 15 + 19 = 169 sacks

citations.quote: the reorder point is made up of the expected demand during the lead time plus the safety stock.
